data.title: Morgan Stanley's Dan Skelly Suggests Stock Market May Have Bottomed
data.description: Morgan Stanley managing director Dan Skelly has indicated that the stock market might have reached its lowest point following a 9% correction. According to NS3.AI, Skelly believes the S&P 500 is likely to have bottomed near 6,300. He also noted that the decline in the VIX, from approximately 30 to 19.48, supports this perspective.

data.title: Italian Prime Minister Criticizes Trump’s Remarks on Pope Leo XIV
data.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has condemned U.S. President Donald Trump's comments regarding Pope Leo XIV, labeling them as "unacceptable." Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the increasing tensions between Meloni and Trump, particularly concerning Trump's aggressive stance on Iran. This disagreement underscores a widening diplomatic gap between Italy and the United States, as Meloni seeks to distance her administration from Trump's controversial foreign policy approaches.

data.title: France's First Quarter GDP Expected to Grow by 0.3%, Says Bank of France
data.description: The Bank of France has projected that the country's GDP will grow by 0.3% in the first quarter. According to Jin10, this forecast reflects the central bank's assessment of the economic conditions in France. The projection comes amid various economic challenges and uncertainties faced by the nation. The Bank of France's outlook is closely watched as it provides insights into the country's economic trajectory.

data.title: ECB's Villeroy Urges Vigilance on Inflation
data.description: European Central Bank Governing Council member François Villeroy de Galhau has emphasized the need for continued vigilance regarding inflation. According to Jin10, Villeroy highlighted findings from recent surveys that suggest inflationary pressures remain a concern. He stressed the importance of monitoring these trends closely to ensure economic stability. Villeroy's comments come amid ongoing discussions within the ECB about monetary policy adjustments to address inflation dynamics in the eurozone.

data.title: Meta Develops AI Version of Mark Zuckerberg for Employee Interaction
data.description: Meta is reportedly developing a photorealistic AI representation of its CEO, Mark Zuckerberg, to facilitate employee communication. According to NS3.AI, this initiative is part of Meta's projected capital expenditure of $115 billion to $135 billion by 2026. The AI system is being trained to emulate Zuckerberg's mannerisms, voice patterns, public statements, and strategic perspectives, aiming to provide a scalable and constantly accessible leadership interface for staff.

data.title: JPMorgan CEO Highlights Blockchain's Efficiency Over Traditional Finance
data.description: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon has highlighted the growing efficiency of blockchain technology compared to traditional financial systems. According to NS3.AI, Dimon noted that blockchain is increasingly becoming faster and more cost-effective, presenting a significant advantage over conventional financial methods. This development underscores the potential of blockchain to revolutionize financial transactions by offering enhanced speed and reduced costs.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview Completes Corporate Network Attack Simulation
data.description: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview has successfully completed a 32-step corporate network attack simulation, marking a significant milestone as the first AI model to achieve this without human assistance. According to NS3.AI, the UK's AI Security Institute reported that the Mythos Preview also attained a 73% success rate in expert-level capture-the-flag tasks. Anthropic has opted to limit access to the model to security research firms rather than releasing it publicly.

data.title: Private Credit Funds Face Significant Withdrawal Requests
data.description: Private credit funds experienced over $20 billion in withdrawal requests during the first quarter, as several managers imposed limits on repurchases. According to NS3.AI, Barings Private Credit Corp., Apollo Debt Solutions, and Ares Strategic Income Fund each restricted withdrawals after redemption requests exceeded 11% of shares. Blue Owl reported that investors aimed to redeem 21.9% of Blue Owl Credit Income Corp. and 40.7% of Blue Owl Technology Income Corp., with both funds capping repurchases at 5%. Moody’s has revised the outlook for Blue Owl Credit Income and the broader BDC sector to negative.

data.title: Nigel Farage Becomes First UK MP to Publicly Purchase Bitcoin
data.description: Nigel Farage has made headlines by purchasing £2 million ($2.5 million) worth of Bitcoin through Stack BTC Plc. According to NS3.AI, this acquisition marks Farage as the first sitting UK Member of Parliament to publicly invest in Bitcoin. Stack BTC Plc revealed that the purchase was financed through capital raises amounting to over £4.2 million ($5.25 million).

data.title: Ukraine's Inflation May Rise Due to Iran Conflict, Central Bank Governor Warns
data.description: Ukraine's central bank governor has warned that the ongoing conflict in Iran could lead to an increase in Ukraine's inflation rate by 1.8 to 2.5 percentage points. According to Jin10, the rise in oil prices, driven by the situation in Iran, is expected to have the most significant impact on Ukraine's inflation and economic growth. Additionally, the increase in fertilizer and production costs is anticipated to have a considerable effect.

data.title: RAVE Liquidations Surge to $6.24 Million, Driven by Short Positions
data.description: RAVE liquidations have reached a total of $6.24 million in the past four hours. According to NS3.AI, this surge was primarily driven by $5.48 million in short liquidations, while long liquidations amounted to $760,000.

data.title: Trump Removes Controversial Social Media Post
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has taken down a social media post that portrayed him in a manner akin to Jesus Christ. Bloomberg posted on X that the image sparked criticism, even among his supporters, leading to its removal.

data.title: Crypto-Backed PAC Invests in Georgia Republican Ahead of Midterms
data.description: Fellowship, a super PAC aligned with cryptocurrency interests, has announced a $300,000 advertising campaign in support of Georgia Republican Clay Fuller as the U.S. midterm elections approach. According to NS3.AI, the PAC claims to have over $100 million from undisclosed donors. In addition to its support for Fuller, the group has endorsed Republican candidates in five other states. Jesse Spiro, Tether's head of government affairs, has been named as the chair of the PAC. This initiative coincides with the ongoing delay of the CLARITY Act in the Senate, which currently lacks a clear path forward.

data.title: Ukraine's Central Bank Aims for 5% Inflation Target in Three Years
data.description: Ukraine's central bank governor has announced that the National Bank of Ukraine is targeting an inflation rate of 5% within the next three years. According to Jin10, this goal is part of the bank's broader strategy to stabilize the country's economy and ensure sustainable growth. The central bank is implementing various monetary policies to achieve this target, aiming to create a stable economic environment conducive to investment and development. The governor emphasized the importance of maintaining a balanced approach to monetary policy to support economic recovery and growth.

data.title: Trump Indicates Potential Focus on Cuba Following Iran Resolution
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has suggested that addressing Cuba could become a priority once the situation with Iran is resolved. According to NS3.AI, Trump's statement highlights a possible shift in focus, although it does not confirm any specific policy action regarding Cuba.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Expected to Speak Soon
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is anticipated to make a public statement shortly. According to NS3.AI, Fox News has reported on the potential timing of this address. Further details regarding the content or context of the speech have not been disclosed.

data.title: RAVE broke through 11 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 253.83%.
data.description: OKX market data shows that RAVE has broken through 11 USDT and is currently trading at 11.12569 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 253.83%.

data.title: In the past 4 hours, RAVE liquidations have exceeded $6 million, mainly affecting short positions.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, RAVE liquidations totaled $6.24 million in the past four hours, including $5.48 million in short positions and $760,000 in long positions.

data.title: Trump: Things won't be pleasant if no deal is reached with Iran within two weeks.
data.description: US President Trump stated that the Strait of Hormuz blockade has begun, and other countries will provide assistance during the blockade, potentially resolving the Iranian issue before oil tankers reach the US. He added that the situation will not be pleasant if an agreement with Iran is not reached within two weeks. The timeline regarding Iran remains unchanged; there is currently no fighting, Iran has no business, and this situation will be maintained. Hopefully, there will be no need to release oil reserves again, but we are ready to take action at any time. (Jinshi)

data.title: RAVE Surges Over 250% in 24 Hours
data.description: RAVE has experienced a significant price increase, surpassing 11 USDT. According to Odaily, the current price is reported at 11.12569 USDT, marking a 24-hour rise of 253.83%.
